tags.name: Allegheny Valley Expressway

tags.description: Built in 1981 so Amtrak's Capitol Limited could reach the station in Pittsburgh. Connects CSX's P&W Subdivision with Norfolk Sothern's Pittsburgh Line. The Allegheny Valley Railroad uses the connector daily to reach NS' Island Ave yard.
tags.name: Bloomfield Connector

tags.description: Single track railroad tunnel. Once had two tracks but one track had to be removed as locomotives and cars increased in size two trains would not be able to fit inside the tunnel. The roof was raised in 2012 to accommodate double stack trains.
tags.name: Pittsburgh Subdivision

tags.description: Opened in 1884 by the Baltimore & Ohio Railroad and upgraded in 1915. It is currently owned and operated by the Allegheny Valley Railroad.
tags.name: W&P Subdivision

tags.description: Part of the Pennsylvania Railroad's Brilliant Branch. Now owned by the Allegheny Valley Railroad. Amtrak train number 43, the Pennsylvanian, turns around on this wye after it drops off the last of its passengers at the station in Pittsburgh.
tags.name: Valley Industrial Track

tags.description: The Valley Subdivision of the Allegheny Valley Railroad. This was built by the original Allegheny Valley Railroad in the 19th century, later bought by the Pennsylvania Railroad and went to Buffalo, NY. Used Sunday through Thursday nights.
tags.name: Allegheny Subdivision

tags.description: Bridge over Alleghney River Boulevard. Built by the Pennsylvania Railroad to carry the existing rail line over the new Allegheny River Boulevard, which opened in 1934.

tags.bridge:name: Brilliant Cutoff Stone Bridge
tags.description: Built by the Pennsylvania Railroad and opened in 1902 as part of the Brilliant Cutoff, 1 of 3 freight bypasses around the busy passenger station in Pittsburgh, and 1 of 2 stone arch bridges on this line.
tags.name: Brilliant Branch
tags.note: Currently owned and operated by the Allegheny Valley Railroad.

tags.destination:lanes: Downtown Pittsburgh|West End Bridge|North Shore|North Shore
tags.name: Beaver Avenue

tags.bridge:name: Ohio Connecting Bridge
tags.description: Built by the Pennsylvania Railroad and opened in 1915, the bridge is currently owned by Norfolk Southern. It connects the Fort Wayne Line at the northern end with the Mon Line at the southern end.
tags.name: Mon Line

tags.description: Single track railroad bridge. Replaced a double track bridge in 1964. The line was originally the mainline of the Pittsburgh & Lake Erie Railroad. Now owned and operated by CSX Transportation.
tags.name: Pittsburgh Subdivision
